utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with costs. It has a description, total monthly_cost, and a cost breakdown. This is fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. It has a description and a monthly_cost. No breakdown required by the format, so this is sufficiently specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. It has a description and a monthly_cost. This is specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. It has a description, monthly_cost, and a breakdown. This is specified. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. It shows monthly = 715 and annual = 715 * 12, which is an unevaluated expression rather than a numeric value. Because it’s not fully specified with a numeric annual cost, this item is not fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan uses descriptive bundles rather than exact product names. We must match them to the closest named options in the ground-truth list. For health coverage: the description mentions enhanced physical health with 24/7 support and concierge, plus advanced mental health and stress management; this aligns best with top-tier PrimeCare Premium Plan or EliteCare Premium Plan for both physical and mental health. Since the plan splits physical (200) and mental (100) and mentions concierge and 24/7, the closest named matches are PrimeCare Premium Plan for physical health (200/month in ground truth) and EliteCare Premium Plan or PrimeCare Premium Plan for mental health; however the mental component at 100/month is below the listed 120/130 for premium options, but the plan’s wording “Advanced Mental Health & Stress Management Plan” with concierge suggests a premium-tier intent. The property coverage described as “Extended EliteHome Protection Plan” most closely maps to EliteHome Premium Coverage. The travel coverage described as “Upgraded Global Executive Travel Plan” with premium medical, pre-existing conditions, evacuation aligns with EliteTravel Premium Plan or PrimeTravel Premium Plan; wording suggests highest tier, so EliteTravel Premium Plan. The additional services are “Premium Insurance Membership” and “Insurance Portfolio Management,” which directly map to the General Services entries with those names.
